title: [firtool] Usage of "-" for default output directory and passes that write files
body:
Going back to at least https://github.com/llvm/circt/commit/0f98ce99d, the default output directory for `firtool` is set to `"-"`.  This can cause weird interactions with passes that are trying to write output files, like export module hierarchy.

E.g., if if you run the following export module hierarchy test through firtool:

```mlir
hw.module @InnerModule(%in: i1) -> (out: i1) {
  hw.output %in : i1
}

hw.module @MainDesign(%in: i1) -> (out: i1) {
  %0 = hw.instance "inner" @InnerModule(in: %in: i1) -> (out: i1)
  hw.output %0 : i1
}

hw.module @TestHarness() attributes {firrtl.moduleHierarchyFile = #hw.output_file<"testharness_hier.json", excludeFromFileList>} {
  %0 = hw.constant 1 : i1
  hw.instance "main_design" @MainDesign(in: %0: i1) -> (out: i1)
}
```

Using `firtool Foo.mlir -verilog -export-module-hierarchy` you wind up with an output in the directory `-`:

```
# tree -- -
-
└── testharness_hier.json

0 directories, 1 file
```

We should do something different here, e.g., the default output directory should be "." or we should interpret "-" to do something different, like emit to stdout and show `// ---- FILE: foobar.json` visual delimiters.
